fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

fusion_size_estimate.method: 3-way inclusion-exclusion over pair estimates: E=E12+E13+E23-2*E123, with E123 estimated from per-dataset overlap probabilities; singleton-aware projection applied for final fused output (include_singletons=True assumption)

singleton_assumption.notes: Conservative singleton-aware estimate: each expected matched cluster reduces the final row count by at least one.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

blocking.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Blocking estimate was conservative; downstream matching/fusion retained more entities than expected.

matching.reasoning: Estimate is moderately off; candidate generation and matching quality likely explain the gap. Matching estimate seems conservative; matcher/fusion retained more clusters than projected.

reasons: Integration report flags a high-severity country_canonicalization_mismatch on release-country, with multiple raw variants (e.g., 'united states of america' vs expected canonical) and a high bundle-like ratio (~13.7%) that will break exact/tokenized comparisons; evaluation confirms very low release-country_accuracy (0.24).

Integration report flags a high-severity list_format_inconsistency across core fields (tracks_track_name/duration/position plus artist and duration) showing mixed encodings (Python list literals, JSON list literals, scalar strings, malformed bracket strings like '[interview]'), which is consistent with catastrophic tracklist accuracies (tracks_track_name_accuracy 0.0; tracks_track_duration_accuracy 0.08).

Evaluation reasoning cites encoding/canonicalization issues (mojibake like 'â€™', 'Ã¨') in track names and other text, which indicates additional text normalization is needed to stabilize comparisons.
normalization_directives.recommendation_summary: Rerun normalization with (1) release-country canonicalized consistently to country names (not mixed long-form/ISO/bundles; split or flag bundle-like values), (2) strict parsing and unification of list-like columns to a single representation (true lists internally; consistent serialization), rejecting/repairing malformed bracket strings, and (3) add a text cleanup pass to repair mojibake/encoding artifacts before tokenization for key text fields (especially tracks_track_name).

reasons: Integration report flags a high-severity country_canonicalization_mismatch on release-country, with multiple raw variants (e.g., 'united states of america' vs expected canonical) and a high bundle-like ratio (~13.7%) that will break exact/tokenized comparisons; evaluation confirms very low release-country_accuracy (0.24).

Integration report flags a high-severity list_format_inconsistency across core fields (tracks_track_name/duration/position plus artist and duration) showing mixed encodings (Python list literals, JSON list literals, scalar strings, malformed bracket strings like '[interview]'), which is consistent with catastrophic tracklist accuracies (tracks_track_name_accuracy 0.0; tracks_track_duration_accuracy 0.08).

Evaluation reasoning cites encoding/canonicalization issues (mojibake like 'â€™', 'Ã¨') in track names and other text, which indicates additional text normalization is needed to stabilize comparisons.
normalization_directives.recommendation_summary: Rerun normalization with (1) release-country canonicalized consistently to country names (not mixed long-form/ISO/bundles; split or flag bundle-like values), (2) strict parsing and unification of list-like columns to a single representation (true lists internally; consistent serialization), rejecting/repairing malformed bracket strings, and (3) add a text cleanup pass to repair mojibake/encoding artifacts before tokenization for key text fields (especially tracks_track_name).

findings.title: Fused size drift vs estimates
findings.description: Actual fused row/ID counts differ from upstream estimates, indicating candidate generation and/or matching retention differences.

findings.title: Potential over-clustering / many-to-many matching patterns in correspondences
findings.description: Correspondence degree distributions suggest non-long-tail behavior and potential over-matching (one-to-many edges / large degrees).

findings.title: Coverage gaps: high missingness in fused output columns
findings.description: Several fused columns are frequently empty, suggesting upstream sparsity, mapping issues, or fusion-rule omissions.

findings.title: Text encoding artifacts (mojibake) detected in key text columns
findings.description: Common mojibake patterns were detected, which can harm matching and attribute comparisons.

findings.title: High rate of mismatches in debug fusion evaluation
findings.description: Debug logs indicate many mismatches and/or missing fused values; likely normalization and parsing issues.

findings.title: Schema/attribute coverage differences across sources
findings.description: Some attributes are present in only a subset of source datasets, which can cause systematic missing fused values.

recommendations.title: Canonicalize country fields consistently (names vs ISO codes; split multi-values)
recommendations.details: Ensure all country-like columns are normalized to a single canonical format (prefer full country names or ISO2/ISO3, but not mixed). Detect and split bundle-like values (e.g., 'US|Canada', 'US/UK', 'US, UK') into lists or separate fields, or flag them for exclusion.

recommendations.title: Unify list-like columns to a single encoding (true lists internally; consistent serialization)
recommendations.details: Detect list-like columns and enforce a single representation. Parse JSON lists and Python literals; repair malformed bracket strings where possible; avoid mixing scalar strings with list encodings in the same column.

recommendations.title: Add text cleanup for mojibake/encoding artifacts before matching/fusion
recommendations.details: Detect common mojibake patterns and repair via consistent decode/encode strategy or library-based fix in preprocessing. Apply to core text fields (names, tracks, artists, labels, genres) before tokenization/comparison.

recommendations.title: Tighten matching thresholds / add disambiguating features to reduce over-clustering
recommendations.details: If correspondence degree distributions show many one-to-many edges or large clusters, increase match thresholds and/or incorporate additional features (release-date, label, duration consistency checks) to prevent over-matching.

recommendations.title: Improve coverage: identify missing fused values and upstream sparsity
recommendations.details: Use debug evaluation logs and missingness scans to locate attributes frequently missing in the fused output. Then address upstream source sparsity or mapping failures (e.g., column mapping, extraction of tracklists).

recommendations.title: Investigate size-estimate drift between expected and actual fused entities
recommendations.details: If actual fused rows/unique IDs differ materially from estimates, inspect candidate generation and match retention. A much larger actual size suggests conservative estimates or under-matching; a much smaller size suggests over-merging.

against validation/test datasets.

-----

normalize_quantity
Parse and optionally convert units + expand scale modifiers
normalize_column
Normalize a pandas Series of quantities
Scale Modifiers

detect_scale_modifier
Detect MEO, MEUR, million, thousand, etc. in text
expand_scale
Expand “5 MEO” → 5,000,000
parse_scaled_number
Parse number with scale modifier
Integrations (External Libraries)

normalize_country, normalize_currency, normalize_language
ISO code normalization via pycountry
validate_stdnum, format_stdnum, normalize_stdnum
Standard number handling via python-stdnum
parse_phone, format_phone, validate_phone
Phone number handling via phonenumbers
validate_email, normalize_email
Email handling via email-validator
Specification & Transformation (New API)

NormalizationSpec
Define how columns should be normalized
ColumnSpec
Per-column normalization settings
transform_dataframe
Apply transformations according to spec
normalize_dataframe
Main entry point with optional auto-detection
Usage Examples

Profile a DataFrame:
>>> from PyDI.normalization import profile_dataframe
>>> profile = profile_dataframe(df)
>>> print(profile.summary())
Normalize quantities:
>>> from PyDI.normalization import normalize_quantity
